whatItDoes: Generates QR codes for WiFi network credentials that can be scanned by phones and tablets to instantly connect to a WiFi network without typing the password.
whoShouldUse: Network administrators setting up guest networks, home users sharing WiFi with visitors, office managers creating QR stickers for meeting rooms, and anyone who wants a quick way to share WiFi access.
whyItExists: Typing long WiFi passwords on mobile devices is error-prone and tedious. WiFi QR codes let guests connect with a single scan, eliminating password entry mistakes and support requests.
useCases: Create a QR code for your home WiFi so guests can connect without asking for the password
Generate a QR sticker for a cafe or office meeting room WiFi
Share a WPA2 enterprise network configuration via QR for quick device onboarding
Print WiFi QR codes for event venues where hundreds of attendees need network access
instructions: Enter the WiFi network name (SSID) in the Network Name field
Type the WiFi password in the Password field
Select the security type: WPA/WPA2, WEP, or Open (no password)
Click 'Generate' to create the QR code
Download the QR code as PNG or copy it to clipboard
Print or share the QR code — anyone can scan it with their phone camera
examples: Input: SSID='MyHomeWiFi', password='secure123!', security=WPA. Output: Scannable QR code that auto-connects devices to the network.
Input: SSID='CoffeeShop', security=Open. Output: QR code for an open WiFi network with no password required.
bestPractices: Use WPA2 or WPA3 security — avoid WEP which is easily cracked
Print QR codes on waterproof paper if placing them in kitchens or outdoor areas
Test the QR code with at least two different phone models before distributing
Include the network name as text below the QR code for troubleshooting
commonMistakes: Typing the wrong SSID — the QR code must match the exact network name including case
Using special characters in the password that may not encode correctly
Forgetting to select the correct security type, which prevents connection
Making the QR code too small to scan reliably — maintain at least 2cm x 2cm when printed
faq: Which phones can scan WiFi QR codes? iOS 11+ and Android 10+ natively support WiFi QR scanning through the default camera app.
Can I embed a WiFi QR code in a webpage? Yes — download the PNG and embed it with an img tag.
Does the QR code work with hidden networks? Yes, but the device will attempt to connect even though the SSID is hidden.
What if the password contains special characters? The tool handles standard WiFi password characters, but test before distributing.
